records.ety: [F. ducat, It. ducato, LL. ducatus, fr. dux leader or commander. See Duke.]

defs: A coin, either of gold or silver, of several countries in Europe; originally, one struck in the dominions of a duke.
notes: ☞ The gold ducat is generally of the value of nine shillings and four pence sterling, or somewhat more that two dollars. The silver ducat is of about half this value.
